full_prompt: Design the primary structural assembly of a CanSat entry for ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26, compliant with the ESERO CanSat Competition Guidelines. The CanSat must fit within a cylindrical envelope of 115 mm height by 66 mm outer diameter; total wet mass must be exactly 350 g (-0/+10 g). The structure comprises a CFRP outer shell (wound 2-ply UD, nominal 0.8 mm wall), a 3D-printed PA12 top nose cap, a 3D-printed PA12 bottom base plate with parachute attachment eyelet, and two internal PCB-mount bulkheads at 30 mm and 75 mm from the base. Material properties: CFRP shell (E 70 GPa, tensile 600 MPa, density 1550 kg/m^3); PA12 (E 1.6 GPa, tensile 45 MPa, density 1010 kg/m^3). The CanSat deploys from a carrier rocket at approximately 1000 m AGL and descends under a parachute rated for minimum 1000 N deployment shock. Descent rate is 8-11 m/s per ESERO rulebook. Load cases: (LC1) launch qualification 20 g axial on 350 g wet mass (applied as distributed body force); (LC2) lateral qualification 5 g on 350 g; (LC3) parachute deployment shock: 1000 N axial at the base-plate eyelet (per rulebook minimum spec); (LC4) ground impact at 10 m/s descent rate: 15 g equivalent static deceleration on the base; (LC5) scrutineering pre-launch handling: 1 kg applied axially at the top nose cap (representing stack weight during integration). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress (or composite Tsai-Wu index for CFRP) must remain below material-appropriate limits: CFRP < 300 MPa (600 / 2.0 FoS), PA12 < 22.5 MPa (45 / 2.0 FoS), across LC1-LC5; (R2) eyelet pull-out: local stress in PA12 near the eyelet insert under LC3 must remain below 22.5 MPa; (R3) structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + bulkheads only, excluding PCBs, battery, parachute) must not exceed 120 g; (R4) first natural frequency must exceed 80 Hz to avoid coupling with carrier rocket motor burn; (R5) envelope compliance: no point of the structure may exceed the 66 mm x 115 mm envelope under any static load case (rigid check). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not required for this basic structural benchmark. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly with shell, nose, base, two bulkheads as separate bodies; composite layup schedule; FEA report per load case; modal report; mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structural assembly of an ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26 entry per ESERO Guidelines. Wet mass exactly 350 g (-0/+10 g) in a 115 x 66 mm cylindrical envelope; deployed from a carrier rocket at ~1000 m AGL, descent 8-11 m/s.

Envelope: 2-ply UD CFRP wound outer shell (~0.8 mm wall); 3D-printed PA12 conical nose cap and base plate with parachute eyelet; two PA12 internal PCB-mount bulkheads at 30 and 75 mm above the base.

Factor regime: material FoS 2.0 (CFRP 300 MPa / PA12 22.5 MPa) via von Mises or Tsai-Wu index; structural dry mass <= 120 g; first natural frequency > 80 Hz; envelope 66 x 115 mm rigid under any static load.

Load cases.
- Launch qualification 20 g axial on 350 g wet, distributed body force.
- Lateral qualification 5 g on 350 g.
- Parachute deployment shock: 1000 N axial at the base-plate eyelet (rulebook minimum).
- Ground impact at 10 m/s: 15 g equivalent static decel on the base.
- Scrutineering pre-launch handling: 1 kg axial at the nose cap (integration stack).

prompt.title: ESA CanSat Europe 2025-26 Primary Structure
prompt.brief: Compliant 66x115 mm 350 g CanSat with CFRP shell + PA12 nose/base, 20 g launch + 1000 N parachute shock.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell for CFRP; TET10 for PA12 nose/base

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short mission (< 10 min) at sea-level conditions.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Descent aero captured via parachute shock LC3.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Thin shell covered by stress gate at this scale.
